title: HTML5, CSS3, & Responsive Web Design
description:
Objective

Master modern semantic HTML5 structures, advanced CSS layout techniques (Flexbox and Grid), and responsive design principles for multi-device compatibility.

Learning Phase

1. Main Topics
  • Semantic HTML5 & Accessibility (a11y)
  • Advanced CSS Flexbox & Grid Layouts
2. Detailed Concepts
  • Media queries and mobile-first workflow
  • CSS custom properties (variables) and pseudo-classes
Learning Resources:
  • MDN Web Docs - CSS – https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS

Practical Implementation

Project Task: Responsive Landing Page

Build a pixel-perfect, fully responsive landing page using semantic HTML5 and modern CSS Grid/Flexbox with mobile-first styling.

Deliverables

  • Live Demo URL (Vercel / Netlify) & Public GitHub Repository URL (MANDATORY / MUST)
  • Source code directory containing well-organized HTML and CSS files.
  • Detailed README explaining setup commands.

Learning Outcomes

Gain hands-on proficiency in structuring clean web layouts that adapt seamlessly across mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports.

title: Modern JavaScript (ES6+) & DOM Manipulation
description:
Objective

Understand asynchronous JavaScript, ES6+ features, and interactive DOM manipulation to build dynamic client-side web behavior.

Learning Phase

1. Main Topics
  • ES6+ Features (Arrow Functions, Destructuring, Spread/Rest)
  • DOM Manipulation & Event Handling
2. Detailed Concepts
  • Asynchronous JavaScript (Promises, async/await, Fetch API)
  • Event bubbling, delegation, and local storage management
Learning Resources:
  • JavaScript.info – https://javascript.info/

Practical Implementation

Project Task: Interactive Web Application (e.g., Weather or Task Tracker)

Build an interactive application that fetches live data from an external public REST API and updates the DOM dynamically based on user input.

Deliverables

  • Live Demo URL (Vercel / Netlify) & Public GitHub Repository URL (MANDATORY / MUST)
  • JavaScript modules implementation handling API requests and DOM elements.
  • Detailed README explaining setup commands.

Learning Outcomes

Master modern scripting practices and asynchronous data handling required for interactive frontend architectures.

title: Advanced JavaScript & Working with APIs
description:
Objective

Deepen advanced JavaScript patterns, closures, prototypes, error handling, and robust RESTful API integration workflows.

Learning Phase

1. Main Topics
  • Advanced JS Patterns (Closures, Scope, Prototypes)
  • RESTful API Consumption & Error Handling
2. Detailed Concepts
  • JSON parsing, HTTP status codes, and try/catch blocks
  • Debouncing, throttling, and performance optimization
Learning Resources:
  • MDN JavaScript Guide – https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Guide

Practical Implementation

Project Task: Dynamic API Explorer App

Create an app that queries data from public APIs, featuring search filters, pagination logic, and error boundaries.

Deliverables

  • Live Demo URL (Vercel / Netlify) & Public GitHub Repository URL (MANDATORY / MUST)
  • Source code with structured API utility services and error handling scripts.
  • Detailed README explaining setup commands.

Learning Outcomes

Acquire advanced scripting logic and resilient error management skills when connecting client apps with remote backends.

title: React.js Fundamentals & Component Architecture
description:
Objective

Learn React fundamentals, component-driven development, state management hooks, and props flow to build reusable user interfaces.

Learning Phase

1. Main Topics
  • JSX, Components, and Props
  • React Hooks (useState, useEffect)
2. Detailed Concepts
  • Component lifecycle and side-effect management
  • Lifting state up and handling user form inputs
Learning Resources:
  • React Official Documentation – https://react.dev/

Practical Implementation

Project Task: React Dashboard or E-Commerce Catalog

Create a modular React application featuring multiple components, dynamic lists, search filters, and reactive state tracking.

Deliverables

  • Live Demo URL (Vercel / Netlify) & Public GitHub Repository URL (MANDATORY / MUST)
  • Modular React application build folder structure.
  • Detailed README explaining setup commands.

Learning Outcomes

Gain professional expertise in building scalable component hierarchies and managing application state efficiently using React.

title: Routing & Advanced State Management (Context API)
description:
Objective

Implement single-page navigation with React Router and handle complex global states cleanly using React Context API.

Learning Phase

1. Main Topics
  • React Router (Nested Routes, Params)
  • Global State Management via Context API
2. Detailed Concepts
  • Avoiding prop drilling with custom hooks and context providers
  • Protected routes and layout wrappers
Learning Resources:
  • React Router Documentation – https://reactrouter.com/

Practical Implementation

Project Task: Multi-Page App with Authentication Flow

Build a multi-page app with React Router, utilizing Context API for user authentication and global theme states.

Deliverables

  • Live Demo URL (Vercel / Netlify) & Public GitHub Repository URL (MANDATORY / MUST)
  • React source files implementing routing configuration and context providers.
  • Detailed README explaining setup commands.

Learning Outcomes

Master multi-view application architecture and state sharing patterns without prop drilling overhead.

title: Tailwind CSS & Modern UI/UX Design Systems
description:
Objective

Style modern React applications efficiently using Tailwind CSS utility classes and design accessible user interfaces.

Learning Phase

1. Main Topics
  • Tailwind CSS Utility-First Framework
  • Responsive Design & Dark Mode Implementation
2. Detailed Concepts
  • Custom configuration, flex/grid utilities, and component styling
  • Building accessible modals, dropdowns, and navigation bars
Learning Resources:
  • Tailwind CSS Documentation – https://tailwindcss.com/docs

Practical Implementation

Project Task: Tailwind UI Dashboard Redesign

Refactor a React application using Tailwind CSS to include responsive grids, card components, and an interactive dark mode toggle.

Deliverables

  • Live Demo URL (Vercel / Netlify) & Public GitHub Repository URL (MANDATORY / MUST)
  • Tailwind configured React app codebase with responsive layouts.
  • Detailed README explaining setup commands.

Learning Outcomes

Gain proficiency in rapid styling workflows and modern UI design systems using Tailwind utility frameworks.

title: Performance Optimization, Forms & API Integration
description:
Objective

Handle complex form validation, manage API requests efficiently, and optimize frontend rendering performance.

Learning Phase

1. Main Topics
  • Advanced Form Validation & Custom Hooks
  • Performance Optimization (Memoization, Lazy Loading)
2. Detailed Concepts
  • React.memo, useMemo, useCallback optimization patterns
  • Form state management and validation libraries
Learning Resources:
  • React Performance Optimization Guide – https://react.dev/learn/render-and-commit

Practical Implementation

Project Task: Optimized Data-Intensive Application

Build a data-heavy search dashboard incorporating memoization hooks to prevent unnecessary re-renders and smooth out form validation loops.

Deliverables

  • Live Demo URL (Vercel / Netlify) & Public GitHub Repository URL (MANDATORY / MUST)
  • Source code optimized with memoization and validation logic.
  • Detailed README explaining setup commands.

Learning Outcomes

Understand how to build production-grade forms and troubleshoot rendering performance bottlenecks in React apps.

title: Capstone Project & Cloud Deployment
description:
Objective

Integrate all learned frontend concepts into a complete, portfolio-ready capstone application deployed live on Vercel or Netlify.

Learning Phase

1. Main Topics
  • End-to-End Frontend Integration
  • Production Build Testing & Cloud Deployment
2. Detailed Concepts
  • Environment variables configuration and build artifact optimization
  • Continuous deployment workflows via GitHub with Vercel or Netlify
Learning Resources:
  • Vercel Deployment Documentation – https://vercel.com/docs

Practical Implementation

Project Task: Capstone React Web Application

Develop a fully functional, multi-feature React app with custom styling, API integrations, and publish the live production link.

Deliverables

  • Live Demo URL (Vercel / Netlify) & Public GitHub Repository URL (MANDATORY / MUST)
  • Fully functional and deployed web application link.
  • Detailed README explaining setup commands.

Learning Outcomes

Successfully demonstrate professional competency in modern frontend engineering by launching a production-grade web project.
